paragraphs.greek: Ο Αλεξ είναι στο μετρό. Βλέπει τα ονόματα των σταθμών: ΣΥΝΤΑΓΜΑ, ΠΑΝΕΠΙΣΤΗΜΙΟ, ΟΜΟΝΟΙΑ.
paragraphs.english: Alex is on the metro. He sees the station names: SYNTAGMA, PANEPISTIMIO, OMONIA.
paragraphs.swedish: Alex är i tunnelbanan. Han ser stationsnamnen: SYNTAGMA, PANEPISTIMIO, OMONIA.
paragraphs.speakerNote: Alex tries reading station names on the metro

paragraphs.greek: Τώρα μπορεί να διαβάσει περισσότερα: πόρτα, βιβλίο, δρόμος. Τα ελληνικά γίνονται πιο εύκολα!
paragraphs.english: Now he can read more: door, book, road. Greek is getting easier!
paragraphs.swedish: Nu kan han läsa mer: dörr, bok, väg. Grekiskan blir lättare!
paragraphs.speakerNote: Alex gains confidence reading Greek words

title.en: More Consonants: Π, Ρ, Τ, Β, Γ, Δ
title.sv: Fler konsonanter: Π, Ρ, Τ, Β, Γ, Δ

explanation.en: Six more consonants! Some look like letters you know but sound DIFFERENT. Π = P (not pi!). Ρ = R (not P!). Τ = T. Now the tricky ones: Β = V sound (not B!). Γ = soft G/Y sound. Δ = 'th' as in 'this' (not D!).
explanation.sv: Sex konsonanter till! Några ser ut som bokstäver du känner igen men låter ANNORLUNDA. Π = P. Ρ = R (inte P!). Τ = T. Nu de knepiga: Β = V-ljud (inte B!). Γ = mjukt G/J-ljud. Δ = 'th' som i engelska 'this' (inte D!).

breakdown.part: β (v) + ι (i) + β (v) + λ (l) + ί (i) + ο (o)

grammarSpotlight.swedishParallel: The biggest traps for Swedish speakers: Β looks like B but sounds like V. Δ looks like a triangle but sounds like English 'th' in 'the'. And Ρ looks like P but is actually R! Practice these three and you'll avoid the most common mistakes.

hint.en: It looks like B but sounds different!
